title: money_conserves() does not span the full tick (post-market mint/burn evades it)
description: Fable review 2026-07-15, correctness risk #2. total_gold_after_step is snapshotted right after the market (mod.rs:12304), but births, birth-stock sufficiency injections, and ignition run AFTER it (mod.rs:12415-12425). FAILURE SCENARIO: a gold mint/burn bug in the birth-endowment or ignition path evades the per-tick money identity in EVERY tick, because each tick re-baselines. It is caught only where a scenario's suite asserts run-long total_gold() constancy (g4b does; not necessarily every suite). Goods, by contrast, ARE covered end-to-end (the after-snapshot at mod.rs:12464-12468 follows births).
acceptance_criteria: Snapshot gold at TRUE end-of-tick (after births/ignition), or add a standing cross-tick gold stitch, so post-market phases are covered by the per-tick money identity everywhere — not only in suites that assert run-long totals. Add/keep a test that a deliberate mint in the birth-endowment path trips the identity. Conservation still holds run-long; goldens byte-identical.

title: Decompose the settlement monolith (sim/src/settlement/mod.rs)
description: Fable review 2026-07-15, top concern #1. sim/src/settlement/mod.rs is ~38,988 lines, ~1,135 functions, 104 feature-gate predicates (*_active()/*_can_run()), and econ_tick() (mod.rs:11886-12502) is a ~616-line pipeline of ~40 gated phases whose ORDERING is semantically load-bearing (role-choice after promotion; provisions before market; births after market but before the conservation snapshot). Extraction has started (burden.rs, closure.rs, birth_gate.rs, wage_labor.rs, share_tenancy.rs) but is mostly NEW-milestone code landing in new files, not decomposition of the monolith — which keeps growing (~+11k since the plan cited ~28k). Behavior-preserving decomposition is 'the price of admission' for further composition (plan review-and-replan-2026-07.md:321-324).
acceptance_criteria: Each extraction is behavior-preserving: flags-off goldens byte-identical; the DH.b 60-cell verdict golden byte-identical; canonical_bytes digest unchanged; full 'cargo test --workspace' green; clippy -D warnings clean. econ_tick phase ordering and the 104 gates preserved exactly. Progress measured by lines/functions moved OUT of mod.rs into cohesive modules with explained seams.

title: Add CI: machine-enforce the goldens/conformance gate
description: Fable review 2026-07-15, top concern #2. There is NO CI (no .github/). The 'goldens gate every change' discipline is entirely operator-process-borne — a single un-run suite before a merge silently voids the guarantee. Add a workflow (plain cargo) running the fast conformance tier per push, and the heavy multi-seed grids (e.g. the ~88s DH.b 60-cell grid) nightly. Must run: cargo test --workspace, cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings.
acceptance_criteria: A CI workflow runs on push/PR and fails on: any test failure, fmt drift, clippy warning, or golden mismatch. Heavy grids scheduled (nightly) rather than per-push. Green on current master.

title: Harden the canonical_bytes digest against under-serialization
description: Fable review 2026-07-15, highest-leverage systemic hazard. canonical_bytes() (mod.rs:26461+) is a hand-maintained, per-flag conditional serialization implementing 'byte-identical iff future behavior identical'. Every gated block requires human reasoning about whether a knob steers a future tick (e.g. forage_yield zeroing at mod.rs:26607, role-choice gate widening at 26488). FAILURE MODE: a new gated phase reads a config knob that steers behavior but is omitted from that flag's digest block -> two behaviorally-divergent settlements digest EQUAL -> the determinism tripwire and every 'byte-identical' regression silently weaken. The per-flag canonical_bytes_include_* tests mitigate for KNOWN fields only.
acceptance_criteria: Structural guard so a new behavior-steering ChainConfig/overlay field cannot be silently omitted from the digest: e.g. generate the gated serialization from a declarative field registry, OR a compile-time/test lint asserting every ChainConfig+overlay field is either digested or explicitly annotated inert. Existing byte-identity tests still green.
